references.text: Leenhouts, PW. 1957. Dichapetalaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 5: 305-316

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 19 April 2025

species.citation: (Roxb.) Engl. , Nat. Pflanzenfam. (Engler & Prantl) 3(4) (1896) 348 ; Leenhouts, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 5 (1957) 310; ≡ Moacurra gelonioides Roxb. , Fl. Ind. (Roxburgh) 2 (1832) 69. ssp. pilosum Leenh., Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 5 (1957) 312. = Dichapetalum holopetalum Merr., PJS 17 (1920) 271; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 388. = Dichapetalum euphlebium Merr., PJS 20 (1922) 394; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 388. Distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Philippines. MINDANAO: Lanao del Sur, Zamboanga, Zamboanga Sibugay, Zamboanga del Norte. Notes: Low elevation thickets and forest up to 670m. Native. ssp. tuberculatum Leenh., Reinwardtia 4 (1956) 82; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 5 (1957) 311. = Dichapetalum monospermum Merr., Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 35 (1906) 34; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 388. Distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. MINDORO, PALAWAN, SIBUYAN. Notes: Low elevation forests. Native.
species.distribution: MINDANAO: Cotabato, Lanao del Sur, Zamboanga, Zamboanga Sibugay, Zamboanga del Norte, MINDORO, PALAWAN, SIBUYAN.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 30 (1926) 270 ; Leenhouts, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 5 (1957) 310.
species.distribution: Moluccas, Nicobar Isls, Philippines. LEYTE, SULU ARCHIPELAGO, TAWI-TAWI.
species.notes: Damp forests at low elevation. Fl. Jul-Aug.

species.citation: (DC.) Boerl. , Handl. Fl. Ned. Ind. (Boerlage) 1 (1890) 199 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 389; Leenhouts, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 5 (1957) 307; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific Biodiv. 17 (2024) 10; ≡ Chailletia timoriensis DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 2 (1825) 57. = Dichapetalum luzoniense Merr. & Rolfe , PJS 3c (1908) 106 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 388. = Dichapetalum olivaceum Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1769. = Dichapetalum submaritimum Elmer , LPB 4 (1912) 1493 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 389. = Dichapetalum robinsonii Merr. , PJS 7c (1912) 280 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 388. = Dichapetalum sericeum Merr. , Sp. Blancoanae (1918) 215 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 388. = Dichapetalum nitidum Merr. , PJS 17 (1920) 270 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 388. = Quilesia sericea Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 177.
species.distribution: Malay Peninsula, New Guinea, Philippines, Solomon Isls. BUSUANGA, DINAGAT (photos), HOMONHON, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Bataan, Batangas, Ilocos Norte, Isabela (photos), Laguna, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Lanao, Surigao, Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PUJADA, SAMAR, SIARGAO, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: Peninsular Malaysia to New Guinea (unrecorded from Sumatra); widespread in the Philippines in most islands and provinces. 0-1800m.

species.citation: (Blanco) Merr. , Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 35 (1906) 35 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 389; Leenhouts, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 5 (1957) 312; ≡ Riana tricapsularis Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 850. = Dichapetalum glabrum Elmer , LPB 2 (1908) 483 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 388. = Dichapetalum ciliatum Merr. , PJS 7c (1912) 279 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 387. = Dichapetalum oblongifolium Merr. , PJS 17 (1920) 271 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 388.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BOHOL, BUCAS GRANDE, CAMIGUIN, CATANDUANES, LEYTE, LUZON: Bataan, Bulacan, Zambales, MINDANAO: ?Bukidnon (photos), Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Sur.
species.notes: Forests and brushwoods. Fl. Mar-Aug; fr. Oct-Jan. 0-1000m.
